Title: Convergent evolution in biosynthesis of cyanogenic defence compounds in plants and insects

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: linamarin and lotaustralin branch from valine/isoleucine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multifunctional P450 converting valine and isoleucine to the corresponding aldoximes via two consecutive N-hydroxylations followed by dehydration, decarboxylation and isomerization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes de novo biosynthesis of linamarin and lotaustralin by generating their oxime precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast with ZfCPR; microsomal enzyme assays with Val/Ile; SPME-GC-MS detection of oximes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP405A2 was the only tested P450 that converted valine and isoleucine into the corresponding oximes. 2. It functions as the first multifunctional enzyme in the insect cyanogenic glucoside pathway. 3. Its activity supports convergent evolution with plant CYP79 function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected predominantly in larval integument microsomes; not detected in haemolymph, gut, or fat body in the quantitative proteomics table
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense against herbivores and predators; secretion in defense droplets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Larvae were analysed while feeding on acyanogenic food plants, forcing de novo cyanogenic glucoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; first committed pathway step
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome pyrosequencing; proteomics LC-MS/MS; PRIDE accession codes 16842-16850
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing insect cyanogenic glucoside biosynthesis in heterologous systems together with CYP332A3 and UGT33A1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional homolog of plant CYP79s but phylogenetically insect-specific; contains a polar substitution in the haem motif linked to amino-acid-to-oxime catalysis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: linamarin and lotaustralin branch from valine/isoleucine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multifunctional P450 converting the aldoximes to the corresponding cyanohydrins via dehydration and C-hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes linamarin and lotaustralin biosynthesis by converting oximes into cyanohydrin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast with ZfCPR; microsomal enzyme assays with oximes; DNPH trapping and LC-MS detection of ketone products released from cyanohydrins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP332A3 was the only tested P450 that converted valine/isoleucine-derived oximes to cyanohydrins. 2. It constitutes the second multifunctional P450 in the insect cyanogenic glucoside pathway. 3. Phylogeny indicates recruitment from insect CYP332 lineage and convergent evolution relative to plant CYP71Es.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in larval integument; lower in gut; detected but not quantified in fat body; not detected in haemolymph
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense against herbivores and predators; secretion in defense droplets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Larvae were analysed while feeding on acyanogenic food plants, forcing de novo cyanogenic glucoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome pyrosequencing; proteomics LC-MS/MS; PRIDE accession codes 16842-16850
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for pathway reconstruction of insect cyanogenic glucoside production in synthetic biology applications
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Corrigendum corrected Fig. 2 label from CYP332A5 to CYP332A3; functional homolog of plant CYP71Es but insect-specific in origin

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; membrane-bound animal UGT with C-terminal membrane anchor and predicted N-terminal ER-targeting transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terminal glucosylation step of linamarin and lotaustralin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates acetone cyanohydrin and 2-butanone cyanohydrin using UDP-glucose to form the cyanogenic glucosides linamarin and lotaustralin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces linamarin and lotaustralin from cyanohydrin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; in vitro assays with [14C]-UDP-glucose and cyanohydrins; TLC detection of radiolabelled linamarin and lotaustralin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT33A1 was the only tested UGT that glucosylated cyanohydrins to form linamarin and lotaustralin. 2. It completes the entire insect cyanogenic glucoside pathway together with CYP405A2 and CYP332A3. 3. It is predicted to be ER membrane-associated with catalytic domain in the ER lumen, distinct from soluble plant UGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in gut and integument microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in gut and integument, including integument where cyanogenic biosynthesis occurs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense against herbivores and predators; secretion in defense droplets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Larvae were analysed while feeding on acyanogenic food plants, forcing de novo cyanogenic glucoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane; catalytic domain predicted in ER lumen
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome pyrosequencing; proteomics LC-MS/MS; PRIDE accession codes 16842-16850
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal enzyme for engineering de novo linamarin/lotaustralin production; should be co-expressed with upstream P450s and ER-compatible system
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Animal-type membrane-bound UGT contrasts with soluble plant cyanogenic UGTs; proposed to participate in insect metabolon organization
